mnemonics: ACID Properties: A C I D (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) - Remember it as 'A Consistent & Isolated Database'.
keyPoints: A Data Model is an abstraction that describes how data is represented and used.
The Relational Model organizes data into tables (relations) of rows and columns.
The Entity-Relationship (ER) Model is a conceptual model used for database design, focusing on entities and their relationships.
commonMistakes: Confusing the 'Database Schema' (the design) with the 'Database Instance' (the actual data at a moment in time).
Using a comma instead of a period to separate table and attribute names (e.g., writing 'Student,Name' instead of the correct 'Student.Name').
